campbell.title: Campbell City Council — May 5, 2026
campbell.summary: The Campbell City Council met to review transportation funding investments from March 2026, adopt a new policy restricting city property use for federal civil enforcement activities, and discuss staffing challenges including job vacancies and employee retention strategies.
keyTopics: Santa Clara Valley Transportation Authority investment report for March 2026
New city policy prohibiting federal civil enforcement on city-owned and operated property
City job openings and efforts to recruit and retain employees

saratoga.title: Saratoga City Council — April 15, 2026
saratoga.summary: The city council held a public hearing to address objections to a plan for cleaning up hazardous conditions on properties identified by the county, and then voted to move forward with the cleanup work. The council also adopted a new technology policy for the city and approved a process for residents to request accommodations.
keyTopics: Public hearing on hazardous property abatement across the city
Approval of county-identified properties for cleanup work
New City Technology Disruption Policy
New process for requesting reasonable accommodations

los-gatos.title: Los Gatos City Council — May 5, 2026
los-gatos.summary: The Los Gatos Town Council met to approve routine administrative items and address vegetation management. The main focus was declaring overgrown brush and hazardous vegetation a public nuisance and authorizing the town manager to sign multi-year service agreements with various vendors.
keyTopics: Hazardous brush and vegetation declared a public nuisance
Approval of landscape and lighting district engineer's report
Five-year service agreements authorized with Pacific and other vendors
April meeting minutes approved

san-jose.title: San José City Council — May 13, 2026
san-jose.summary: The San José City Council held a brief meeting on May 13, 2026 to review and prepare upcoming agendas. The council finalized items for their May 19 meeting, confirmed the meeting schedule for the next fiscal year, and handled appointments to various city committees and boards.
keyTopics: Finalized May 19 City Council agenda with any necessary time assignments
Approved Fiscal Year 2026-2027 meeting schedule
Made City Council appointments to committees, boards, and commissions
Processed travel request from Councilmember Foley

mountain-view.title: Mountain View City Council — May 14, 2026
mountain-view.summary: This was a notice about how to participate in Mountain View City Council meetings. Residents can submit questions before meetings, and all agendas and meeting locations are posted at least 24 hours in advance for boards, commissions, and committees.
keyTopics: Submit questions online before meetings using the provided link
All meeting agendas and locations posted 24 hours in advance
Contact City Manager's Office at 650-903-6301 or CNC@MountainView.gov with questions

sunnyvale.title: Sunnyvale City Council — May 5, 2026
sunnyvale.summary: The Sunnyvale City Council held a special closed-door meeting on May 5, 2026, followed by a regular public council meeting. The specific business discussed in the closed session was not disclosed to the public, as is standard practice for confidential matters.
keyTopics: Closed session meeting held (details not public)
Regular council meeting convened
Small business matters on agenda

cupertino.title: Cupertino City Council — May 11, 2026
cupertino.summary: The city council held an initial review session on Cupertino's proposed budget for fiscal year 2026-27. The council also discussed requirements for people who lobby the city to register and report their activities.
keyTopics: FY 2026-27 budget proposal review
Lobbying registration and disclosure requirements under city code
Public testimony procedures for legislative matters

santa-clara.title: Santa Clara City Council — May 14, 2026
santa-clara.summary: The May 14, 2026 Santa Clara City Council meeting included a legal notice informing residents about lawsuit deadlines. If you want to challenge a city decision made by the council, you have 90 days from the final decision to file a lawsuit, as required by California law.
keyTopics: 90-day deadline to file lawsuits challenging city decisions
California Code of Civil Procedure Section 1094.6 requirements
Legal notice on quasi-adjudicative decisions

milpitas.title: Milpitas City Council — May 5, 2026
milpitas.summary: The Milpitas City Council held a meeting to plan the city's spending for the next five years and discuss the upcoming year's operating budget. The council also approved a master plan for parks and trails in the Innovation District and updated their internal procedures handbook.
keyTopics: 5-year capital improvement plan (2027-2031) - what infrastructure projects the city will fund
2026-27 operating budget presentation - how the city will spend money day-to-day
Innovation District Parks and Trails Master Plan - new park and trail development
City Council procedures handbook updates

palo-alto.title: Palo Alto City Council — May 7, 2026
palo-alto.summary: The City Council held a hearing on a proposal to build a 6-story, 72-unit affordable housing building at 450 Lytton Avenue on a city-owned parking lot. The project would be 100% affordable and is requesting special concessions under state affordable housing laws.
keyTopics: 72 affordable housing units in a new 6-story building
Project replaces 52-space city parking lot (Lot T)
Developer requesting density bonus concessions under state law
Small lot size (0.43 acres) for large project
